Notice Of Motion And Motion For Monetary And Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-27-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT AMERICAN MEDICAL RESPONSE WEST's (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS "AMERICAN MEDICAL RESPONSE AMBULANCE SERVICE, INC.") Motion For Monetary And Terminating Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion for terminating sanctions is denied in its entirety.

"A trial court must be cautious when imposing a terminating sanction because the sanction eliminates a party's fundamental right to trial, thus implicating due process rights." (Lopez v. Watchtower Bible and Tract Society of New York, Inc. (2016) 246 Cal.App.4th 566, 604.) There is no basis for a terminating or evidentiary sanction since plaintiff cured the cited non-compliance. While Mr. Buckley's declaration fails to comply with CCP 2015.5, there is no dispute that plaintiff's counsel ultimately paid the sanctions that were required pursuant to
this court's September 17, 2024, order. (Reply, 2:5 ["Plaintiff would not have paid the sanctions but for this motion."].)

While plaintiff should have paid the previously ordered monetary sanctions prior to this motion being filed, defendant's effort to leverage that non-payment into grounds to terminate this case lacked substantial justification, warranting the denial of monetary sanctions to defendant on this motion.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, with a cover letter stating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/HEK)

Off Calendar Jan-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT AMERICAN MEDICAL RESPONSE WEST'S (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS "AMERICAN MEDICAL RESPONSE AMBULANCE SERVICE, INC.") Motion For Undertaking [Code Civ. Proc. 1030].

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
J. Lloyd Buckley, Esq., of the Beverly Law Firm, (310) 552-6959, for Plaintiff.
Damon M. Thurston, Esq., of Rankin, et al., (510) 433-2600, for Defendant.

The matter is ARGUED. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant's motion for an undertaking per CCP 1030 is denied. To prevail on this motion, defendant must establish that plaintiff resides out of state and "there is a reasonable possibility that the moving defendant will obtain judgment in the action." (CCP 1030(b).) Defendant fails to show a reasonable possibility of obtaining judgment. The evidence discloses that after plaintiff made his lane change, defendant's driver accelerated. (Buckley Decl., Ex. C [Yamaguchi Depo., 50:17-51:15].) There is therefore evidence of comparative fault and it is unlikely that the moving defendant will obtain judgment in its favor.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling, in compliance with CRC 3.1312, and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org.
